turns.prompt: Design a SEBI compliance monitoring software system that tracks trading activities, reports anomalies, and maintains an immutable audit log.

services.name: API Gateway
services.responsibility: Single entry point for all external and internal requests, handles authentication, authorization, routing, and rate limiting.

services.name: Auth Service
services.responsibility: Manages user authentication (Compliance Officer, System Administrator, Auditor) and authorization (RBAC).

services.name: UI Service
services.responsibility: Provides the web-based dashboard for compliance officers, rule configuration interface, and report viewing.

services.name: Data Ingestion Service
services.responsibility: Connects to external trading platforms, ingests raw trading activity data, performs initial validation, and publishes data to an event stream.

services.name: Rule Configuration Service
services.responsibility: Manages the definition, versioning, and lifecycle of SEBI compliance rules, allowing configuration and updates.

services.name: Rule Engine Service
services.responsibility: Subscribes to trading activity data and rule updates, evaluates activities against defined compliance rules in near real-time, and publishes evaluation results (violations, compliant events).

services.name: Anomaly Detection Service
services.responsibility: Subscribes to trading activity data and rule evaluation results, applies advanced analytics and machine learning models to detect complex anomalies or potential rule violations beyond explicit rules.

services.name: Audit Log Service
services.responsibility: Subscribes to all significant system events (ingested data, rule evaluations, anomalies, user actions, system decisions) and writes immutable, cryptographically secured records to the Ledger Database.

services.name: Notification Service
services.responsibility: Subscribes to anomaly and violation events, generates alerts, and sends notifications to relevant stakeholders via configured channels (e.g., Email, SMS).

services.name: Reporting Service
services.responsibility: Generates compliance reports based on SEBI guidelines, provides data for the UI dashboard, and supports ad-hoc queries for compliance officers.

services.name: Configuration Service
services.responsibility: Manages general system settings and parameters not directly related to compliance rules.

communication: REST/gRPC: Synchronous communication for UI interactions, API Gateway routing, and service-to-service calls requiring immediate responses (e.g., rule configuration, dashboard data retrieval).

Kafka/RabbitMQ (Event-Driven Architecture): Asynchronous communication for high-volume data streams (trading data ingestion, rule evaluation results, anomaly events, audit events), ensuring decoupling, scalability, and resilience.
persistence: PostgreSQL (Relational Database): For managing user accounts, roles, rule definitions, system configurations, report metadata, and other transactional data requiring strong consistency.
Time-Series Database (e.g., InfluxDB, AWS Timestream): For storing high-volume trading activity data, optimized for time-based queries and high write throughput, supporting real-time monitoring.

Immutable Ledger Database (e.g., AWS QLDB, Hyperledger Fabric): For maintaining the cryptographically secured and immutable audit log of all trading activities, rule evaluations, anomalies, and system actions, ensuring data integrity and non-repudiation.

Redis (In-memory Data Store/Cache): For caching frequently accessed data (e.g., active compliance rules for the Rule Engine, dashboard metrics, user sessions), improving performance and reducing database load.
deployment: Kubernetes Cluster: Orchestration for containerized microservices (Docker), providing auto-scaling, self-healing, service discovery, and load balancing.

Managed Cloud Services: Leveraging cloud provider services for databases (e.g., AWS RDS for PostgreSQL, AWS Timestream, AWS QLDB), message brokers (e.g., AWS MSK for Kafka), caching (e.g., AWS ElastiCache for Redis), object storage (e.g., AWS S3 for reports), and API Gateway (e.g., AWS API Gateway).
Containerization (Docker): All services are deployed as Docker containers.
scalability: Horizontal Pod Auto-scaling (HPA) in Kubernetes: Automatically scales stateless services (Data Ingestion, Rule Engine, Anomaly Detection, UI, Reporting) based on CPU utilization, memory, or custom metrics (e.g., message queue depth).
Asynchronous Processing with Message Queues: Decouples producers from consumers, buffers spikes in data ingestion, and allows independent scaling of processing services.
Database Sharding and Replication: For Time-Series and PostgreSQL databases to distribute load, improve read/write performance, and ensure high availability.
Caching with Redis: Reduces load on primary databases by serving frequently accessed data from high-speed memory.
Load Balancers: Distribute incoming traffic across multiple instances of services and the API Gateway.
Stateless Service Design: Most services are designed to be stateless to facilitate easy horizontal scaling and resilience.
